steps.title: Set the discriminant to zero
steps.content:
A quadratic has exactly one real solution when its discriminant is $0$. For $-3x^2+24x+c=0$, use $a=-3$ and $b=24$:
$$b^2-4ac=0$$

steps.title: Solve for the constant
steps.content:
Substitute the values and solve:
$$24^2-4(-3)c=0$$
$$576+12c=0$$
$$12c=-576$$
$$c=-48$$
The answer is B.
